author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
Status update (2026-06-22).

`7100b51` (issue-type-and-slug-fields) closed. v2.1 schema adds
`IssueType` enum and `slug: Option<String>` to `IssueRecord`,
with `Storage::resolve(handle)` accepting either a 7-hex id or a
slug across every id-taking CLI verb. Spec bumped to v2.1
(`docs/storage-format.md` §3, §3.x, §5.2, §5.7); new error
kinds `invalid_slug` / `slug_collision` / `slug_not_found`
documented in `docs/cli-json.md`. Migration recipe applied to
all 10 existing tickets; 212/212 workspace tests green.

Commits: `5f30706` (storage + CLI), `98f7dba` (docs sweep).

What's still open under this epic: the remaining mvp-storage
scope items, but the load-bearing schema work is done — `jjf
ready` (`69d5e1b`) is now unblocked and is next.

author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
Status update (2026-06-22).

Filed `b9f628b` `storage-scale-index` (research) under this
epic. Captures the read-path scale problem and the design
call between (a) snapshot cache only, or (b) snapshot cache
plus a real index, with rusqlite-bundled vs redb as the
index candidates.

Why now: every list-shaped read today spawns one `jj log`
per issue (~50-100ms). We're nowhere near the cliff at
~17 issues but the shape is locked in; capturing the
verdict before the data layer drifts further.

Hard "not Dolt" preference noted — jj already gives us a
versioned/mergeable/distributed substrate; the index, if we
ship one, is purely derived state at `.jj/jjforge.<ext>`,
rebuildable from the op chain, .gitignored.

author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
2026-06-23: Research ticket `487536a storage-out-of-tree-refs`
closed. Verdict: move every issue and memory off the `issues`
jj bookmark to per-issue git refs under `refs/jjf/issues/<id>`
and `refs/jjf/memories/<key>` (git-bug's pattern). Kills HEAD
drift at the source — the 4-CLI write dance is replaced by
`git update-ref` CAS.

Design doc at `docs/storage-out-of-tree.md`. Implementation
epic filed as `bd98097` with 8 sketched child tickets (write
path, read path, init, v2→v3 migrator, push/pull refspec,
snapshot cache, test sweep, self-host guard removal). Serial
dispatch, ~3–4 sessions.
